channel.title: Eubello - Thoughtful Writing
channel.description: Mezbaul's blog on operations, systems, workflows, and practical writing.

item.title: Small websites still matter
item.description: In a web full of platforms, feeds, and heavy templates, a small website still feels valuable because it can be direct, personal, and fully owned by the person making it.

item.title: What I actually want from a simple piece of tech
item.description: Most of the technology I keep using is not the loudest or most ambitious. It is the technology that stays clear, predictable, and easy to trust after the first excitement disappears.

item.title: Things travel teaches you that routine cannot
item.description: Routine teaches steadiness, but travel teaches a different kind of attention. It interrupts assumptions, rearranges scale, and makes even ordinary moments feel newly visible.

item.title: How operational clarity saves technical time
item.description: Teams usually feel technical pressure first, but many delays begin somewhere quieter: unclear requests, missing ownership, scattered information, or a process that only exists in memory.

item.title: A simple approach to support requests and follow-through
item.description: Support work becomes frustrating when requests arrive in fragments, ownership is unclear, and updates live only in private conversations. A simpler system usually works better than a bigger one.

item.title: What makes a practical SOP actually useful
item.description: A procedure is not valuable because it is long or formal. It is valuable when someone can use it during real work without needing a second explanation.
